_comment: Built-in flavor defaults for the Scryer preview. Copy this file and point scryer.flavorConfigPath at your copy to override any values. Later layers win per key: built-in default → built-in per-flavor → user default → user per-flavor.

default._comment_wow: WoW engine constant: UIParent is always 768 logical units tall; width is derived from screen aspect ratio.
default._comment_rendering: WoW rendering calibration values. See inline notes for derivation.
default._comment_fontLetterSpacing: WoW's DirectWrite renderer produces ~6.3% wider advance widths than the browser's ClearType renderer for the same font file. Calibrated against FRIZQT__.TTF at height=12 by measuring 'Example Bare Frame' (18 chars): WoW=151px vs browser=142px at 125% DPI → 7.2 CSS px gap → 0.4px per char → 0.4/12 = 0.033em. Change this when using a non-default font.
default._comment_autoFontSizeRatio: Fallback when no explicit FontString size is set: CSS font-size = frame height × this ratio.
default._comment_fontSmoothing: Maps to -webkit-font-smoothing. 'antialiased' (default) matches WoW's DirectWrite grayscale AA; browser default on Windows is ClearType (subpixel), which makes text appear heavier.
default._comment_viewport: Preview viewport background. The checkerboard pattern indicates transparent areas.
default._comment_ruler: Pixel ruler appearance. rulerSize is the strip thickness in CSS px. Intervals are in WoW logical pixels.
default._comment_rulerShadow: Dark halo behind ruler labels so they stay legible over any canvas content.
default._comment_statusBar: Status bar appearance (the fixed bar at the top of the preview panel).
default._comment_placeholder: Placeholder tiles shown for unresolved textures. Hue is deterministic per path via djb2 hash (seed 5381); saturation/lightness set the muted tone so placeholders are visually distinct but not distracting.
default._comment_layout: Layout solver parameters. epsilon is the floating-point tolerance used when checking whether two anchor point-fractions differ on an axis; maxIterations caps the iterative dependency-resolution pass.
default._comment_sandboxTimeout: Per-call Lua execution timeout in milliseconds. Each JS→Lua call (doString, event dispatch) is killed if it exceeds this limit, preventing infinite loops from hanging VS Code. Applies per-call, not to the total session.
default._comment_onUpdate: OnUpdate tick loop. onUpdateHz is the target tick rate (ticks per second). onUpdateTimeout is the per-tick Lua execution budget in milliseconds; a tick that exceeds this is killed with a warning.
